text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers check each other’s work, they often catch bugs early, before the code reaches production. This saves time and reduces costly fixes later. Additionally, code reviews encourage knowledge sharing within the team. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, while everyone gains a better understanding of different parts of the project. Over time, this practice improves overall team quality, as coding standards are consistently applied and mistakes are minimized. Ultimately, regular code reviews build stronger, more reliable software and create a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement.
